content.rendered:
Continuing the 7-Eleven series, we turn to one of the 11 different drivers to reach the podium so far in 2026: Speedworks Corolla Racing's Josh Cook. The 21-time race winner has taken a single trip to the rostrum this year, coming at Oulton Park.

Oulton Park Island

Cook's podium arrived in Race 1; the Toyota Corolla GR Sport driver locked in a battle for the lead from the first lap alongside race winner Dan Cammish (NAPA Racing UK) and Tom Ingram (Team VERTU). The #66 held his own through a safety car restart that reset the race to 16 laps, but couldn't find a way past Cammish at the front, eventually settling for second at the flag, his first podium of the campaign.

Cook sits eighth in the Drivers' Championship as the mid-season break comes to a close, with the grid back in action at Thruxton (25/26 July) this weekend.

title.rendered: Speedworks Corolla Racing ready to resume at UK's fastest circuit

excerpt.rendered: The 2026 Kwik Fit British Touring Car Championship returns to action this weekend at the high-speed Thruxton Circuit, with Speedworks Corolla Racing looking to build on the encouraging performances shown before the summer break. Following a productive first half of the season, the Cheshire-based squad arrives at one of the championship's fastest and most demanding […]

content.rendered:
The 2026 Kwik Fit British Touring Car Championship returns to action this weekend at the high-speed Thruxton Circuit, with Speedworks Corolla Racing looking to build on the encouraging performances shown before the summer break.

Following a productive first half of the season, the Cheshire-based squad arrives at one of the championship's fastest and most demanding venues determined to convert its evident pace into another strong haul of championship points.

The weekend also marks an exciting new chapter for the team, as Ryan Bensley makes his Speedworks Corolla Racing debut alongside multiple BTCC race winner Josh Cook.

Thruxton has long been regarded as one of Cook's strongest circuits. The Bath driver has enjoyed considerable success around the Hampshire venue throughout his BTCC career, earning the nickname "King of Thruxton" thanks to a series of memorable victories and front-running performances over recent seasons.

With the Toyota Corolla GR Sport continuing to demonstrate impressive race pace throughout 2026, Cook heads into the weekend full of confidence as he targets another strong points haul in front of what is expected to be another capacity crowd.

For Bensley, the weekend represents the fulfilment of another career milestone. Following his appointment during the championship's summer break, the talented driver will make his first competitive appearance for Speedworks Corolla Racing, having already impressed during previous BTCC outings.

While adapting to a new team, new engineers and a new car presents an understandable challenge, Bensley arrives with confidence after previously enjoying success at Thruxton, giving him valuable knowledge of one of Britain's quickest racing circuits.

The summer break has also provided valuable time for the team to regroup, analyse the opening half of the campaign and prepare for the busy run of races that now lies ahead.

With both drivers eager to get back behind the wheel and the entire team refreshed following the short break, anticipation is high as the second half of the 2026 season gets underway.

Christian Dick, Team Principal, Speedworks Corolla Racing, said:"It's great to finally be going racing again after the summer break. Everyone has worked incredibly hard behind the scenes over the last few weeks and there's a real sense of excitement throughout the team heading into Thruxton.

"Josh has always been exceptionally strong there and it's a circuit that really suits both his driving style and the characteristics of the Corolla. He's earned the reputation as the 'King of Thruxton' for good reason, and we're looking forward to seeing what we can achieve together this weekend.

"We're also delighted to welcome Ryan for his first race weekend with the team. He's settled in brilliantly already and we're all excited to see him out on track. There'll naturally be plenty to learn as he gets familiar with the car and the team, but we're confident he'll adapt quickly and show the pace we know he's capable of.

"The first half of the season demonstrated the potential of our package. Now it's about maintaining that momentum, continuing to improve and making the most of every opportunity over the remaining rounds."

Josh Cook, Driver, Speedworks Corolla Racing, said: "It's always special racing at Thruxton. I've enjoyed some fantastic moments there over the years and it's definitely one of the circuits I look forward to most on the calendar.

"The Corolla has shown strong pace this season and hopefully we can hit the ground running after the summer break. It's a circuit where confidence is everything, so we'll be looking to maximise every session and put ourselves in the mix from the outset."

Ryan Bensley, Driver, Speedworks Corolla Racing, said: "I genuinely can't wait to get started. Joining Speedworks Corolla Racing is a fantastic opportunity and everyone's made me feel incredibly welcome from day one.

"Thruxton is actually one of my favourite circuits and somewhere I've enjoyed success before, so to make my debut with the team there makes it even more special.

"There's obviously a lot to learn with a new team and a new car, but I'm looking forward to working closely with Josh and the engineers, building confidence throughout the weekend and hopefully putting it all together."

content.rendered:
Continuing the 7-Eleven series, we turn to one of the seven different race winners so far in 2026: NAPA Racing UK's Dan Cammish. The Yorkshireman has taken three podiums from the first 12 races, including a maiden win of the campaign at Oulton Park, to sit third in the standings.

Donington Park

Cammish's first podium of the season came in Race 2, running fourth in the early laps before finding the pace to move forward. On lap 16, the #52 Ford Focus Titanium swept around the outside of Charles Rainford (WSR) at Redgate, and although the BMW 330i M Sport driver fought back, Cammish had the position secured by the Old Hairpin. The #27 held on to complete a NAPA Racing UK one-two behind teammate Ash Sutton.

Oulton Park Island

Cammish's maiden win of the season arrived at Oulton Park, on the back of pole position earned after sitting behind the penalised Árón Taylor-Smith (Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport) to inherit Saturday's Qualifying Race victory. Leading from lights to flag in Race 1, the #27 fended off a persistent Josh Cook (Speedworks Corolla Racing) through a safety car restart to take the chequered flag, his first BTCC victory of the year. He backed it up with third in Race 2, running strongly on hard tyres, a feat not seen in recent years through the UK's premier motorsport series.

Cammish currently sits third in the Drivers' Championship as the mid-season break comes to a close, with the grid back in action at Thruxton (25/26 July) this weekend.

title.rendered: Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport returns to action at high-speed Thruxton

excerpt.rendered: Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport heads to Thruxton, Hampshire, this weekend for event five of the 2026 Kwik Fit British Touring Car Championship. With the summer break coming to an end, the team returns to action looking to continue its fight at the sharp end of both the overall and Independent championships. Following the […]

content.rendered:
Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport heads to Thruxton, Hampshire, this weekend for event five of the 2026 Kwik Fit British Touring Car Championship. With the summer break coming to an end, the team returns to action looking to continue its fight at the sharp end of both the overall and Independent championships.

Following the six-week summer break, Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport is well placed in both championship standings, sitting fifth in the Overall Teams’ Championship and third in the Independent Teams' Championship, just 16 points adrift of the lead after a strong start to the 2026 campaign.

The team has claimed two outright race victories this season, one of only two teams to have secured multiple race victories so far. Árón Taylor-Smith was responsible for the first, delivering a masterclass in challenging conditions at Brands Hatch to secure his first overall BTCC victory in more than a decade. Gordon Shedden then added to the team's tally with a scintillating race three victory at a sweltering Snetterton, further underlining the credentials of the three-time champion.

Despite the frustrations of the most recent round at Oulton Park, the MB Motorsport Corolla continued to demonstrate its pace. Taylor-Smith started the Qualifying Race from the front, with a storming qualifying performance on Saturday to set the fastest time of the weekend, losing out on pole position to a safety car restart penalty in the Race to Pole. The #52 would then convert that Saturday speed into an Independent race victory in the weekend's opening contest. Shedden also enjoyed a strong Sunday, recording a trio of points-scoring finishes as the team continued to demonstrate encouraging progress.

In the Drivers' Championship, Scotsman Gordon Shedden currently sits ninth, having already amassed three times as many points as he had at the same stage of the 2025 season. Taylor-Smith occupies eleventh, with his points tally already more than double what he had achieved by this point last year.

In the Independent Drivers' Championship, the pair sit fifth and seventh respectively as they look to strengthen their championship positions at Thruxton this weekend (25/26 July).

Thruxton has long been a happy hunting ground for Shedden, with "Flash" claiming an impressive seven victories and eleven podium finishes during his illustrious BTCC career. Taylor-Smith is also no stranger to success at the Hampshire circuit, having secured a hat-trick of top-three Independent finishes there in 2024.

Following the 50-minute practice session (Sat, 10:25), the all-important Qualifying Groups (Sat, 14:00) and Qualifying Race (Sat, 15:05) will set the grid for Sunday’s racing action.

Sunday’s races are scheduled for 11:45, 14:40 and 17:30, with all three races available to watch live on ITV4 and ITVX in the UK, Racer Network in North America, and the BTCC YouTube channel anywhere else in the world.

Árón Taylor-Smith (#50) said: "We head to Thruxton, which is one of my favourites, after the summer break with plenty of positives to build on from the first half of the season. Collecting my first BTCC win in more than a decade and heading the field in qualifying have undoubtedly been the highlights, but we've also shown on numerous occasions that we have the pace to challenge at the front of the field. Everyone at Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport has been working incredibly hard behind the scenes to ensure we're in the best possible position as we resume our 2026 campaign, and our aim is to carry the momentum we've built so far into the second half of the season."

"Thruxton is a circuit that demands not only the right car setup, but also complete commitment behind the wheel. It's a track I've enjoyed strong results at in the past, so I'm looking forward to getting back out there and carrying our momentum into the weekend. Hopefully we can put together three strong races, score some big points, and continue to demonstrate what this team is capable of."

Gordon Shedden (#52) said: ”It’s great to be back in action after some time away from the track. We’ve shown on several occasions what we’re capable of, with the victory at Snetterton being a particular highlight for me. Alongside that, we’ve collected Independent wins and podium finishes, and despite a few ups and downs, we’ve continued to make strong progress in both championships with the Corolla."

"Thruxton is a unique circuit where outright speed has to be balanced with tyre management, so getting the setup right will be absolutely crucial. It’s a track I’ve always enjoyed and one that holds plenty of great memories, so if we execute the weekend well, there’s every reason to believe we can come away with a strong haul of points."

Mark Blundell, Sporting Director at Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport, said: "The 2026 season has delivered plenty of highs and lows for everyone at Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport, but we head into the second half of the campaign in a strong position, firmly in contention in both championships. Árón and Gordon have both produced some outstanding performances, claiming a race victory apiece alongside a strong collection of Independent wins and podium finishes. As we head to Thruxton, our focus is on building on the momentum from the opening four rounds and returning to the fight for silverware following a challenging weekend at Oulton Park."

"Everyone at Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport has worked incredibly hard throughout the summer break to ensure we're ready to hit the ground running. Now it's about maintaining that focus, continuing to work as one team, and taking another step forward at Thruxton this weekend."

title.rendered: PM Junior Championship prototype set for public debut at Thruxton BTCC weekend

excerpt.rendered: Visitors to this weekend’s Kwik Fit British Touring Car Championship event at Thruxton will have the opportunity to see the prototype PM Junior Championship car in person for the very first time. Marking another significant milestone in the build-up to the inaugural 2027 season, the championship will showcase both the prototype race car and its […]

content.rendered:
Visitors to this weekend’s Kwik Fit British Touring Car Championship event at Thruxton will have the opportunity to see the prototype PM Junior Championship car in person for the very first time.

Marking another significant milestone in the build-up to the inaugural 2027 season, the championship will showcase both the prototype race car and its M-Sport engine, giving prospective drivers, families, teams and fans an exclusive first look at Britain’s newest junior racing series.

The PM Junior Championship display will be located in front of the Thruxton Centre, where members of the championship team will be on hand throughout the weekend to answer questions and provide information on the series, including driver eligibility, technical specifications, running costs and the opportunities available for young drivers looking to take the next step in their motorsport careers.

Designed for drivers aged 14-17, the PM Junior Championship has been created to provide a clear, professional and cost-controlled pathway into circuit racing. Competing on the support bill of the Kwik Fit British Touring Car Championship, the series combines high-performance machinery with a controlled technical specification to deliver close, competitive racing while keeping costs manageable.

The prototype on display features the core elements that will define the championship car, including an M-Sport developed engine, a Quaife sequential gearbox and differential package, and a Cosworth electronics system, all supplied by technical partners with extensive experience at the highest levels of motorsport.

Visitors interested in learning more about the PM Junior Championship are encouraged to visit the display throughout the weekend, where the team will be available to discuss the championship, the car and the opportunities available to prospective drivers, teams and commercial partners.

Looking ahead, preparations are also underway for the prototype to make its first public on-track demonstration during a Kwik Fit British Touring Car Championship event later this season, giving fans the opportunity to see and hear the car in action ahead of the championship’s debut in 2027.

For championship and commercial enquiries during the Thruxton weekend, representatives from the PM Junior Championship team will be available throughout the event.

Interested in learning more?

Prospective drivers, parents, teams and commercial partners are invited to visit the PM Junior Championship display throughout the weekend or arrange a dedicated meeting with the championship team.

To book a meeting or register your interest, visit the PM Junior Championship here and fill in the available form.

content.rendered:
Continuing the 7-Eleven series, we turn to one of the 11 different drivers to reach the podium so far in 2026: Team VERTU's Tom Chilton. Now in his 20th BTCC season and with well over 500 race starts to his name, the #3 Hyundai i30 Fastback N picked up his solitary podium of the campaign so far at Snetterton.

Snetterton 300

Chilton kept himself firmly in the podium picture throughout Sunday's Race 2, running strongly in the leading pack and always within touching distance of the rostrum. With teammate Ricky Collard (Team VERTU) already through to second after a move around the outside of Dan Cammish (NAPA Racing UK) into Brundle on lap eight, Chilton pounced at the same spot a lap later, this time up the inside, showing the pace and racecraft to make the move stick and complete a Team VERTU two-three, his first top-three finish of the 2026 season.

Chilton sits just outside the top ten in the Drivers' Championship as the mid-season break comes to a close, with the grid back in action at Thruxton (25/26 July) this weekend.

title.rendered: Expanded line-up for WSR as BTCC resumes at Thruxton

excerpt.rendered: WSR resume the challenge for the 2026 Kwik Fit British Touring Car Championship with an expanded three-car line-up as the UK’s leading series returns from its summer break at Thruxton this weekend (25/26 July). Joining BTCC race winners Charles Rainford (#99) and Daryl De Leon (#2) for the rest of the season aboard a trio of BMW 330i […]

content.rendered:
WSR resume the challenge for the 2026 Kwik Fit British Touring Car Championship with an expanded three-car line-up as the UK’s leading series returns from its summer break at Thruxton this weekend (25/26 July).
 
Joining BTCC race winners Charles Rainford (#99) and Daryl De Leon (#2) for the rest of the season aboard a trio of BMW 330i M Sports will be Scottish newcomer Lewis Gilbert (#29), who is switching to touring car racing from single-seater racing.
 
The 18-time title winners hold second place in the overall and Independent Teams’ championships after a fast and consistent start to the season, the highlights of which have been two victories for Rainford and two podiums for De Leon.
 
The #99 car is fourth in the Drivers’ points table after victory at Oulton Park, which was the third of his BTCC career. The 28-year-old from Horsham now returns to a circuit where he was a race winner in Porsche Carrera Cup GB in 2023.
 
Team-mate De Leon holds second place in the Independents’ standings – only five points behind the leader – and sixth in the overall championship.
 
The Anglo-Filipino, who is based in Cambridgeshire, was the Jack Sears Trophy winner for the weekend at Thruxton last year as he surged towards securing the title later in the season.
 
22-year-old Gilbert, from Dalry, North Ayrshire, has never raced at Thruxton and had his first taste of the BMW 3 Series BTCC car with a one-day test at Snetterton a week before the Hampshire event.
 
The Scottish driver is a UK and European Champion in karting and is joining WSR half a season into his maiden season of car racing.
 
In total, the team has won there nine times in the BTCC and significantly sealed BMW’s 100th series victory in 2017, as well as double wins with the 3 Series in 2019 and 2024.

Charles Rainford, #99 driver, WSR, said: "The Summer break has come earlier than normal this year because of the World Cup, and our wins at Snetterton and Oulton Park feel like an awfully long time ago, but we’ve had a good chance to assess where we are and plan strategically for the rest of the season. Thruxton is unique in the BTCC because so much of it is high-speed and because we don’t have the variable of option tyres on race day. The BMW seems to have a mixed history at Thruxton, so it’s hard to know where we’ll shake out, but if we work to the very best of our ability, we should be somewhere in the mix."
 
Daryl De Leon, #2 driver, WSR, said: "The summer break’s been very busy, quick and brilliant! I'm just ready to get down to business now at Thruxton and to activate my yournameonabtcccar.com campaign. I’m sixth in the championship and second in the Independent Drivers’ points, so I’m going to continue pushing on to climb the standings. Thruxton is traditionally a front-wheel-drive track so we’ll definitely have to dig deep if we want the success with the BMW however, we know that’s what it will take so that will be our plan."
 
Lewis Gilbert, #29 driver, WSR, said: "Ever since I agreed to join WSR for the remaining six events of the year, I’ve been counting down the days to my debut. The BTCC is 100 per cent where I want to race and to finally be here, with a positive first test at Snetterton in the bag, feels great. Touring Cars is a totally new game for me so I know I have a lot to learn and I’m not setting any outlandish targets for the weekend, but having the experience of the WSR team around me is going to be a huge help. I can’t wait to get going at Thruxton."

title.rendered: 7-Eleven: Gordon Shedden

excerpt.rendered: Continuing the 7-Eleven series, we turn to the most successful active driver in the championship: Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport's Gordon Shedden. The three-time champion has made his move to the Independent squad pay off with two podiums from the first 12 races, one of them a win, his first in the chrome blue. […]

content.rendered:
Continuing the 7-Eleven series, we turn to the most successful active driver in the championship: Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport's Gordon Shedden. The three-time champion has made his move to the Independent squad pay off with two podiums from the first 12 races, one of them a win, his first in the chrome blue.

Donington Park

Shedden marked his Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport debut with a podium on race day one; the #52 Toyota Corolla GR Sport crossed the line third in Race 2 behind both NAPA Racing UK’s race winner Ash Sutton and Dan Cammish. The result also secured an Independents' class victory, a strong start for a team which was only in its first event running two cars.

Snetterton

The three-time champion improved on his season's best result in the weekend finale at Snetterton, delivering a defensive masterclass from pole position. The Scot held off an early challenge from the Cataclean Plato Racing pairing of Daniel Rowbottom and Adam Morgan, before four-time champion Sutton and two-time champion Tom Ingram (Team VERTU) both closed in over the second half of the race. Shedden would lead the trio home as nine championships stood on the podium. The #52 refused to be moved, taking the flag for his 54th career win, his first for Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport, and a second Independents' class victory of the day.

Shedden sits ninth in the Drivers' Championship as the mid-season break comes to a close, with the grid back in action at Thruxton (25/26 July) this weekend.

title.rendered: Where to Watch: Thruxton

excerpt.rendered: The 2026 Kwik Fit British Touring Car Championship returns from its mid-season break at Thruxton this weekend (25/26 July) for the fifth event of the year to bookend the first half of the campaign. After three different victors at Oulton Park, the BTCC heads to the UK's fastest circuit as Ash Sutton (NAPA Racing UK) […]

content.rendered:
The 2026 Kwik Fit British Touring Car Championship returns from its mid-season break at Thruxton this weekend (25/26 July) for the fifth event of the year to bookend the first half of the campaign.

After three different victors at Oulton Park, the BTCC heads to the UK's fastest circuit as Ash Sutton (NAPA Racing UK) leads the way for a helter-skelter weekend of action around the 2.36-mile circuit.

The weekend gets underway with Free Practice, then we go straight into qualifying. Two groups of 15-minute sessions, split according to the finishing order from Free Practice between odd and even finishing positions. The group with the fastest time will take the odd-numbered grid spots for the Qualifying Race. 15 minutes after qualifying, the pit lane opens ahead of the ‘Race for Pole’. An eight-lap race will then take place with half points awarded, as the result forms the grid for the first of the traditional trio of races on Sunday.

Last time out, the 2026 grid headed to Cheshire for the fourth race weekend of the championship. After another sensational Saturday, culminating in the all-new ‘Race to Pole’, Dan Cammish (NAPA Racing UK) would take his maiden Qualifying Race victory, setting Sunday up for the perfect start as the Ford Focus Titanium driver then took his first victory of the season. Championship leader Sutton would stand triumphant in Race 2, with a performance that saw the four-time champ win by the biggest margin seen in 27 years. The final race of the weekend saw Charles Rainford (WSR) take his third career victory, becoming only the second multiple winner in 2026.

This weekend’s race day action will feature on the home of the BTCC, ITV4, from 1100 on Sunday (26 July), while the ITV YouTube page will host the Qualifying Race and Qualifying on Saturday (25 July) from 1355.

As in 2025, each race day of the season – including all support series’ races – will be streamed LIVE around the globe on the BTCC YouTube channel, with the exception of the UK and North America, which have existing broadcast arrangements.

The BTCC will be aired LIVE in North America (US and Canada), continuing the multi-year agreement with the RACER Network, streaming live and on demand as part of a long-term agreement with the championship.

All 30 races are scheduled to air on the main RACER channel, whilst support series’ and extended BTCC highlights will be available to watch via the RACER+ App, the network’s premium motorsports streaming platform. Race 1 will begin at 06:45 Eastern Time (ET) with Race 2 following at 09:40 ET and Race 3 closing out the day’s racing at 12:30 ET.

Click here for the full TV guide for Thruxton

title.rendered: TOCA and ITV agree new multi-year television contract

excerpt.rendered: Live BTCC coverage and extensive highlights to continue until at least 2030 TOCA – organiser of the British Touring Car Championship – has today (21 July) announced a new three-year television agreement with ITV, extending its UK free-to-air broadcast partnership through to at least the end of the 2029 season. The new contract commences in […]

content.rendered:
Live BTCC coverage and extensive highlights to continue until at least 2030


TOCA – organiser of the British Touring Car Championship – has today (21 July) announced a new three-year television agreement with ITV, extending its UK free-to-air broadcast partnership through to at least the end of the 2029 season.

The new contract commences in 2027 and sees ITV continue to broadcast the BTCC live, free-to-air on ITV4, plus its on-demand streaming service ITVX, providing some seven hours of live coverage from each race day.

Every Saturday qualifying session will also continue to be shown live online via the ITV YouTube channel, giving access to fans worldwide. Extensive highlights will also remain across ITV4, ITVX and (when schedules permit) ITV1, with all main race day and highlights programming available on-demand via ITVX.

Introduced in 2025, the all-day live race-day coverage will continue worldwide via the BTCC YouTube channel, excluding the UK and North America which have live broadcast agreements. The BTCC will also share additional video content online, including ‘as live’ race clips being posted across social media.

This latest agreement represents one of the most significant free-to-air BTCC broadcast packages on record. In total, the championship will boast more than 400 hours of coverage per season across the ITV Network contract and the BTCC’s YouTube channel.

ITV has aired the UK’s premier and most prestigious motorsport championship since 2002, and this latest extension will take the partnership to an astonishing 27 successive years – ensuring this relationship remains one of the longest, and strongest, in UK television and British sporting history.

The BTCC’s popular support series will also continue to receive the same live coverage and their own dedicated highlights programming across the ITV network.

In summary, this new agreement provides the same level of free-to-air ‘all day live’ coverage, including all highlights and on-demand programmes, on the same channels as present, including

  • 10 x 7hr live broadcasts per channel
  • 10 x 90min BTCC highlights programmes
  • 34 x 60min support race highlights
  • UK and global live streaming of BTCC qualifying via YouTube
  • Global live-streaming of the full race-day on YouTube (outside of the UK/North America)

Specifically:

  • All main race day broadcasts will be shown live on ITV4 and ITVX
  • Saturday qualifying streamed live on the ITV YouTube channel
  • BTCC highlights will be shown on ITV4, ITVX and (when schedules allow) ITV1
  • Support Race highlights will be shown on ITV4
  • Full BTCC race day coverage will be available, on demand, on ITVX and ITV Sport YouTube

Niall Sloane, ITV Director of Sport, said: “This new deal continues our long-standing commitment to broadcast the very best British Touring Car Championship action, free-to-air, for the UK audience on ITV.

“Our partnership with the BTCC spans nearly three decades and we look forward to the upcoming seasons delivering an extensive package of coverage for our viewers to enjoy across ITV, ITVX and YouTube.”

Alan Gow, BTCC Chief Executive, said: “I’m extremely pleased and proud to have finalised our new contract with the ITV Network, thus continuing our excellent and continually expansive broadcast partnership for the BTCC and its support championships into an incredible 27th year.

“This new contract underlines my unwavering commitment to continue to keep the BTCC as freely accessible to as many people as possible… rather than for it to be hidden behind a paywall. My policy has always been for the BTCC to have the widest reach achievable, and our long relationship with ITV makes that happen.

“In fact, the BTCC is the only UK motorsport that has live, free-to-air coverage of an entire championship on mainstream linear television… and, thanks to our great partners at ITV, long may that continue.”

title.rendered: 7-Eleven: Charles Rainford

excerpt.rendered: Continuing the 7-Eleven series during the mid-season break, we turn to a driver who's built on his breakthrough rookie season: WSR's Charles Rainford. The Horsham driver has taken three podiums from the first 12 races, two of them wins, and is alongside Ash Sutton (NAPA Racing UK) as the only drivers so far in 2026 […]

content.rendered:
Continuing the 7-Eleven series during the mid-season break, we turn to a driver who's built on his breakthrough rookie season: WSR's Charles Rainford. The Horsham driver has taken three podiums from the first 12 races, two of them wins, and is alongside Ash Sutton (NAPA Racing UK) as the only drivers so far in 2026 to have won more than once.

Donington Park

Rainford's season began building on the foundations of his debut season with a podium finish. The #99 BMW 330i M Sport ran strongly throughout Race 1, holding off the chasing pack before Sutton and Mikey Doble (LKQ Euro Car Parts with Power Maxed Racing) both found a way through late on, the latter completing the move on the run to the final chicane. Rainford's pace had been strong enough to stay well clear of the battle for fifth when Cataclean Plato Racing's Adam Morgan was handed a post-race penalty for a minor overboost infringement, restoring third place to Rainford.

Snetterton 300

The victory breakthrough came at Snetterton, where Rainford's BMW finally suited the conditions, having already topped Saturday's Qualifying session and leading from lights to flag in the Qualifying Race. The #99 made a clean getaway from pole in Sunday's opener and resisted the early pressure to build a decisive two-second gap, one he preserved across the full 12 laps for his first win of 2026 and only the second of his career. Teammate Daryl De Leon backed it up with third to complete a double podium, WSR's best weekend of the season so far.

Oulton Park Island

Rainford added a second win of the season in the reverse-grid finale at Oulton Park. Starting third on soft tyres, he used the pace advantage to sweep around the outside of polesitter Chris Smiley (Restart Racing) at Cascades after a Safety Car restart to take the lead. Ricky Collard (Team VERTU) hounded the Horsham driver when the field closed up, at one stage closing to within a second, but Rainford held his composure to take the flag 1.319 seconds clear, his second win of the season and third of his career.

The WSR driver sits fourth in the Drivers' Championship across the mid-season break. The #99 and the rest of the grid return to action at Thruxton (25/26 July).

title.rendered: 7-Eleven: Daryl De Leon

excerpt.rendered: Continuing the 7-Eleven series during the mid-season break, we turn to one of the 11 different drivers to reach the podium so far in 2026: WSR's Daryl De Leon. The reigning Jack Sears Trophy champion, who took his maiden BTCC win at Brands Hatch last season, has gone twice to the podium so far this […]

content.rendered:
Continuing the 7-Eleven series during the mid-season break, we turn to one of the 11 different drivers to reach the podium so far in 2026: WSR's Daryl De Leon. The reigning Jack Sears Trophy champion, who took his maiden BTCC win at Brands Hatch last season, has gone twice to the podium so far this year, both results coming at the same event.

Snetterton 300

De Leon's first podium of the campaign arrived in Sunday's opener, at what amounts to a home event for the Cambridge-based driver. Starting sixth, the #2 BMW 330i M Sport made steady progress through the opening laps before catching and passing Josh Cook (Speedworks Corolla Racing) for third, a position he held to the flag as teammate Charles Rainford controlled the race up front for a WSR double podium.

The Anglo-Filipino racer backed it up in the weekend finale, crossing the line third behind race winner Gordon Shedden (Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport) and a fast-recovering Ash Sutton (NAPA Racing UK), 3.226 seconds off the win. The double podium capped a Snetterton weekend that took his weekend points total to 41, his best haul of the season, and lifted him to third in the Independents' Trophy.

De Leon sits sixth in the Drivers' Championship as the mid-season break comes to a close, with a first outright win of 2026 still the target when the field returns to action at Thruxton (25/26 July) this weekend.

title.rendered: BTCC returns at Fast & Furious Thruxton

excerpt.rendered: UK's fastest circuit sets the scene following mid-season break The 2026 Kwik Fit British Touring Car Championship returns from its traditional mid-season break, as the UK’s premier motorsport spectacle heads to Thruxton (25/26 July) to reignite the championship fight. Ash Sutton (NAPA Racing UK - Ford) sits 48 points atop the drivers' standings after an […]

content.rendered:
UK's fastest circuit sets the scene following mid-season break

The 2026 Kwik Fit British Touring Car Championship returns from its traditional mid-season break, as the UK’s premier motorsport spectacle heads to Thruxton (25/26 July) to reignite the championship fight.

Ash Sutton (NAPA Racing UK - Ford) sits 48 points atop the drivers' standings after an impressive display at Oulton Park bookended a never-before-seen start to a season. Despite the imposing introduction to the campaign, Tom Ingram (Team VERTU - Hyundai) closed the gap to Sutton last time out, standing on the podium in every race at the Cheshire venue. The two-time and reigning BTCC champion will look to add to his three previous victories in Hampshire to continue closing in on the top spot.

Dan Cammish (NAPA Racing UK - Ford) opened race day last time out with a commanding lights-to-flag victory in the day’s first exchange, in a weekend that saw the #27 jump from fourth to third in the drivers’ standings. The second bout went to Cammish’s teammate Sutton, who took victory by over 19 seconds, the largest margin of any win in 27 years.

Charles Rainford (WSR - BMW) was the next driver to take a win, becoming only the second driver behind Sutton to take multiple victories this season over the first 12 races. Chris Smiley (Restart Racing - Hyundai) started the race on pole, but it was always going to be a tall order to hang on at the front with a gaggle of Goodyear soft tyre runners behind him. Rainford's BMW 330i M Sport fired off the line and was quickly into second on the approach to Old Hall Corner for the first time.

After a safety car period, Rainford immediately got a great exit from Old Hall on the restart and completed a daring move around the outside of Smiley’s Hyundai before they reached Cascades.

Across the grid it is both NAPA Racing UK and Alliance Racing/Ford that lead the way in both the Teams’ and Manufacturers’/Constructors’ championships. LKQ Euro Car Parts with Power Maxed Racing currently holds a slender one-point advantage over WSR in the Independent Teams’ standings.

As it is in the teams, Mikey Doble (LKQ Euro Car Parts with Power Maxed Racing - Audi) holds a narrow lead of five points over Daryl De Leon (WSR - BMW) in the Independent Drivers’ order, whilst Doble’s stablemate Dexter Patterson (Steel Seal with Power Maxed Racing - Audi) leads the Jack Sears Trophy.

Now, the BTCC has left one of the most picturesque and challenging venues on the calendar, the 2.23-mile Oulton Park Island Circuit, endured its mid-season break, and arrives at the UK’s fastest circuit, Thruxton. The Hampshire venue is a staple for speed on the BTCC calendar, and we are ready to get back on track!

Cook’s Crown?

Josh Cook (Speedworks Corolla Racing - Toyota) has the most victories of any driver at Thruxton with ten, holding a connection with the UK’s fastest circuit unlike anyone else at any other venue.

Despite this, it has been four years since the #66 took victory in Hampshire, and after a difficult start to the season, the 21-time race winner will be heading to his home circuit looking to recreate his success.

Cook will be heading to Thruxton with a new teammate, as Speedworks Corolla Racing announced the signing of Ryan Bensley for the remainder of the season. The three-time race starter replaces Max Buxton, who has taken on a new Driver Development & Reserve Driver role within the organisation.

Bensley is not the only new face as the BTCC returns, as WSR confirmed it is expanding to a three-car lineup for the rest of the season, with Scotsman Lewis Gilbert joining the most decorated team in BTCC history.

TV, Timetables and Tickets

Complementing the tin top racing will be a sensational line-up of support series, as the Porsche Carrera Cup Great Britain, F4 British Championship certified by FIA, MINI CHALLENGE Trophy, Mini Se7en Challenge and Mini Miglia Challenge complete the schedule of exciting races.

BTCC will be wowing the crowds both trackside and at home, with Saturday's 'Race to Pole' and the earlier qualifying session being streamed live via ITV Sport's YouTube Channel from 1355, before ITV4's seven-and-a-half-hour race day broadcast gets underway at 1100 on Sunday.

Remember, continuing what started for the first time in the championship's history in 2025, the BTCC is being broadcast globally with every race being streamed live in 2026 via the BTCC YouTube Channel, whilst fans in North America can watch the action on the Racer Network. Look out for the races on Sunday starting at 1145, 1440 and 1715 (BST).

There's no place like being there of course, and fans can purchase tickets in advance or on the gate throughout the weekend – visit here for more details.

title.rendered: 7-Eleven: Tom Inrgam

excerpt.rendered: Continuing the 7-Eleven series during the mid-season break, we turn to the reigning champion's title defence: Team VERTU's Tom Ingram. After a rocky start to the season that yielded no points from the opening two races at Donington Park, the #80 Hyundai i30N has taken seven podiums from the remaining ten races, including a maiden […]

content.rendered:
Continuing the 7-Eleven series during the mid-season break, we turn to the reigning champion's title defence: Team VERTU's Tom Ingram. After a rocky start to the season that yielded no points from the opening two races at Donington Park, the #80 Hyundai i30N has taken seven podiums from the remaining ten races, including a maiden win of the campaign, to sit second in the standings.

Donington Park

Ingram's year did not start strong despite obvious pace between the Bucks-born driver and the Hyundai i30 N with the #80 crossing the line first in Race 1 only to be disqualified for a significant overboost infringement. The next time the Team VERTU star would head out on track, a failing alternator forced him into the pits at the end of the formation lap in Race 2, leaving him without a result from either of the two opening exchanges. The two-time champion responded in Race 3, working his way through the field from a lowly grid position to join a three-way battle with teammate Ricky Collard (Team VERTU) and Dan Cammish (NAPA Racing UK) by lap 13. Ingram briefly lost third before reclaiming it and then passed Collard with ease to move into second, and although he couldn't close the 4.4-second gap to race winner Ash Sutton (NAPA Racing UK) in the closing laps, he salvaged a podium from an otherwise miserable weekend.

Brands Hatch Indy

Ingram was back on the rostrum in Race 1, finishing third as Collard completed a Team VERTU double podium behind race winner Sutton. His maiden win of the season followed in the weekend finale. Starting fifth, Ingram made a superb getaway to pass Sutton on the run to Clearways at the end of the opening lap, with polesitter James Dorlin (Restart Racing) holding Sutton in his path. Ingram then hunted down and dispatched Dorlin for the lead soon after, and controlled the remainder of the race to take the flag 3.848 seconds clear.

Snetterton 300

Ingram's podium run continued in Race 1, where he finished on the rostrum behind race winner Charles Rainford (WSR), having earlier got the better of Josh Cook (Speedworks Corolla Racing) off the line to move into second place. It was as good as his weekend got, however, as an alternator failure in Race 2 sent him from the lead to retirement inside the closing laps, managing a good recovery drive to eighth in the weekend finale.

Oulton Park Island

Ingram made his way onto the podium in all three races at Oulton Park, closing in on title leader Sutton in the process. After he finished third in the opener as Cammish took a maiden win in 2026, the reigning champion finished second in Race 2 as Sutton romped clear for a dominant victory. The reverse-grid finale saw Ingram complete the set, finishing third behind winner Rainford and teammate Collard.

Ingram sits second in the Drivers' Championship in the mid-season break, 48 points behind Sutton. The #80 and the rest of the grid return to action at Thruxton (25/26 July).

title.rendered: Speedworks Corolla Racing confirms driver change ahead of Thruxton

excerpt.rendered: Ryan Bensley to join squad for remainder of the 2026 BTCC season Speedworks Corolla Racing has confirmed a change to its driver line-up ahead of the resumption of the 2026 Kwik Fit British Touring Car Championship at Thruxton next weekend (25/26 July). Ryan Bensley will take over the Toyota Corolla GR Sport for the remainder […]

content.rendered:
Ryan Bensley to join squad for remainder of the 2026 BTCC season

Speedworks Corolla Racing has confirmed a change to its driver line-up ahead of the resumption of the 2026 Kwik Fit British Touring Car Championship at Thruxton next weekend (25/26 July).

Ryan Bensley will take over the Toyota Corolla GR Sport for the remainder of the season, whilst Max Buxton will move into an expanded Driver Development & Reserve Driver role within the organisation.

The move forms part of a wider long-term strategy and allows Buxton to remain fully integrated within the Speedworks programme, continuing his development both on and off track, which will help prepare him for a return to the grid at the earliest appropriate opportunity.

Since making his BTCC debut, Buxton has demonstrated maturity, professionalism and a strong work ethic. The young driver will continue supporting engineering development, simulator work, driver coaching within the team's Rising Stars programme and manufacturer activities, alongside fulfilling reserve driver responsibilities throughout the remainder of the season.

Bensley, who impressed during his BTCC appearances in 2025, arrives at Speedworks with enormous enthusiasm and determination to establish himself on the BTCC grid. His performances last season demonstrated both natural pace and an ability to learn quickly, while his professional attitude, technical feedback and willingness to work closely with engineers marked him out as one of the championship's most promising young talents.

With Thruxton presenting the fastest and arguably one of the most demanding circuits on the calendar, Bensley is eager to begin work with the team and maximise every opportunity available throughout the remainder of the campaign.

Christian Dick, Team Principal, Speedworks Corolla Racing, said: "Firstly, I'd like to thank Max for the professionalism he's shown throughout this process. He's an important part of our programme and that certainly doesn't change with this announcement.

"We've always viewed driver development as a long-term journey, not simply what happens over a handful of race weekends. Max will continue to play a valuable role within the team, working closely with our engineers, coaching our Rising Stars Junior programme and remaining fully involved in our BTCC operation as Reserve Driver.

“At the same time, we're absolutely delighted to welcome Ryan to Speedworks Corolla Racing. He impressed everyone with his performances last year, and we've been following his progress closely. He's quick, technically minded, works exceptionally well with Josh, and we're excited to see what we can achieve together during the remainder of the season."

Max Buxton, Driver Development & Reserve Driver, Speedworks Corolla Racing, said: "I’ve loved the first part of the year and yes, naturally, every racing driver wants to be on the grid, but I’ll continue to keep working away behind the scenes. I'm grateful to Speedworks for continuing to invest in my development.

"This certainly isn't the end of my BTCC journey – in many ways it's just another step along it. The goal is to be back on the grid as soon as possible, and I'll be using this opportunity to keep learning, keep developing and make myself an even stronger driver when that chance comes.

"I'll still be with the team at every event, supporting Ryan and Josh, working alongside the engineering team, supporting the team’s Rising Stars programme and contributing wherever I can. I'm determined to keep improving and make sure I'm ready when the opportunity arrives."

Ryan Bensley, Driver, Speedworks Corolla Racing, said: "I'm incredibly excited to be joining Speedworks Corolla Racing and I'd like to thank Christian, Danny and everyone involved for the opportunity.

“Credit to Max, who I think showed great courage stepping into the BTCC at such a competitive time, and to hold his own was remarkable given his short racing career prior to joining.

“It’s a dream to drive for Speedworks Corolla Racing! Competing in the BTCC has been my life goal, but to also drive alongside Josh Cook – who has been so instrumental in my career to date, gives me the best platform to learn quickly ensuring we have the best chance to succeed as a team this year and next. Thruxton will be a massive challenge, but it’s actually my favourite track and with the new format, I can’t wait to get stuck in.”

title.rendered: Teams Championship: The story so far

excerpt.rendered: Four events into the 2026 Kwik Fit British Touring Car Championship, NAPA Racing UK sits at the top of the Teams Championship in the mid-season break. Behind it, the fight has been tense, with three squads sharing the same points total at one stage of the campaign. Donington Park The Alliance Racing-run outfit set the […]

content.rendered:
Four events into the 2026 Kwik Fit British Touring Car Championship, NAPA Racing UK sits at the top of the Teams Championship in the mid-season break. Behind it, the fight has been tense, with three squads sharing the same points total at one stage of the campaign.

Donington Park

The Alliance Racing-run outfit set the tone from the opening round, its Ford Focus Titanium matched only on points by the Audi A3 Saloon of LKQ Euro Car Parts with Power Maxed Racing. NAPA Racing UK pulled clear across the remaining two rounds at Donington Park, posting the biggest points tally of any team in both, while WSR came away ahead of Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport after the two teams were tied on points throughout rounds one and two. It left NAPA Racing UK with a healthy lead after the opening weekend, with LKQ Euro Car Parts with Power Maxed Racing second and the Surrey-based squad third.

Brands Hatch

NAPA Racing UK collected the most points of any team in the opening race once more, with Team VERTU splitting the Fords and the BMWs for a strong second on points. LKQ Euro Car Parts with Power Maxed Racing hit back to post the best team tally in round two, ahead of NAPA Racing UK, before the EXCELR8 Motorsport-run outfit took the most points of the round in round three. The upshot heading out of Kent was one of the tightest midfield battles of the season: WSR, Team VERTU and Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport all left Brands Hatch locked together on the same points total for third in the championship.

Snetterton

WSR posted the best team tally of the opening round at Snetterton, after taking pole position in the Qualifying Race, with Team VERTU second on points. NAPA Racing UK bounced back to top the points in round two, the Mercedes A35 Saloon of Cataclean Plato Racing enjoying its best result of the season with the second-highest team tally. WSR and the Alliance Racing-run outfit were tied for the most points once more in round three, with Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport's Toyota Corolla GR Sport also on the pace. It left LKQ Euro Car Parts with Power Maxed Racing and Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport separated by a single point for fourth in the standings leaving Norfolk.

Oulton Park

NAPA Racing UK posted the biggest points tally of any team in both round one and round two at Oulton Park, with Team VERTU and WSR the closest challengers across the weekend. The Surrey-based squad closed out the event with the best team tally of round three. It capped an event that saw NAPA Racing UK extend its advantage at the head of the Teams Championship, while Team VERTU's consistency across the four events carried the Hyundai i30 N squad up to third overall, with WSR second.

NAPA Racing UK's advantage is a significant one heading to Hampshire, but the battle for the remaining places on the road has been anything but settled, with WSR, Team VERTU, LKQ Euro Car Parts with Power Maxed Racing and Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport all still very much in the mix. The Kwik Fit British Touring Car Championship resumes at Thruxton (25/26 July).

title.rendered: 7-Eleven: Árón Taylor-Smith

excerpt.rendered: Continuing the 7-Eleven series during the mid-season break, we turn to one of the seven different race winners so far in 2026: Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport's Árón Taylor-Smith. Unlike most of the names in this series, the Irishman's story isn't one of building consistency; it's a single, unforgettable afternoon in the rain that […]

content.rendered:
Continuing the 7-Eleven series during the mid-season break, we turn to one of the seven different race winners so far in 2026: Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport's Árón Taylor-Smith. Unlike most of the names in this series, the Irishman's story isn't one of building consistency; it's a single, unforgettable afternoon in the rain that stands as his only visit to the podium so far this season.

Brands Hatch Indy

Taylor-Smith arrived at Brands Hatch off the back of a difficult season opener, starting Race 2 down in 13th as intermittent rain turned to a downpour through race day. As the track worsened, the Toyota Corolla GR Sport came alive, with the Irishman carving through half the field in a car that looked transformed in the wet, climbing to second before catching and passing Ricky Collard (Team VERTU) down the main straight to take the lead. Ash Sutton (NAPA Racing UK) also found a way past the fading Collard moments later, but couldn't close the gap to the flying Corolla before contact between Charles Rainford (WSR) and Tom Ingram (Team VERTU) at Druids brought out a red flag and halted any chance of the gap being reeled in, which sealed the result. It was Taylor-Smith's first BTCC win in ten years, his last having come at Rockingham back in 2016, and the fifth victory of his career.

The Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport driver looks toward Thruxton still chasing a follow-up to that afternoon, with the Brands Hatch triumph remaining his only podium of the campaign so far. Despite this being the case, a strong showing at Oulton Park just before the mid-season break shows Taylor-Smith and the Toyota Corolla GR Sport have more pace to show. The #50 and the rest of the grid return to action at Thruxton on 25 and 26 July.

title.rendered: Power Maxed Racing expands to four-car entry for first time in its history

excerpt.rendered: Power Maxed Racing will expand to a four-car entry in the 2026 Kwik Fit British Touring Car Championship, with Nick Halstead returning to the team for the second half of the season. The expansion marks the first time that the Warwickshire-based team will field four cars in its BTCC programme, strengthening its presence on the […]

content.rendered:
Power Maxed Racing will expand to a four-car entry in the 2026 Kwik Fit British Touring Car Championship, with Nick Halstead returning to the team for the second half of the season.

The expansion marks the first time that the Warwickshire-based team will field four cars in its BTCC programme, strengthening its presence on the grid in the UK’s premier motorsport championship.

Halstead rejoins Power Maxed Racing having previously competed with the team during the 2025 season. Following that campaign, the British driver relocated to Australia, where he remained active in motorsport. He now returns to the BTCC with a familiar outfit as part of PMR’s expanded line-up.

The team’s first four-car appearance will come next month at the Knockhill race weekend of the 2026 season (8/9 August).

Power Maxed Racing will mark the moment as a triumph and celebration as Race Day comes exactly 12 months to the day, since the horrific fire that devastated the team’s base. Famously, the squad scrambled to get its cars and kit together in under a week, with the support of the entire motorsport community, and made it onto the grid in Scotland.

This time around, however, Power Maxed Racing will be arriving at the much-loved circuit in Fife with four brand new Audi A3 Saloons.

Adam Weaver, Team Principal, said: “Expanding to four cars is a significant step for Power Maxed Racing and reflects the continued progress the team has made in recent seasons. It’s a credit to everyone involved, both on and off-track.

“When we looked at the opportunity to add a fourth entry, Nick was a natural fit. He understands how the team operates, integrated well in 2025, and has continued to build experience since then. With it also commemorating the anniversary of the fire, with Nick providing so much support in getting back racing this time last year, it’s very fitting that he is getting in the car at Knockhill. We’re pleased to welcome him back and look forward to working with him again.”

Nick Halstead said: “I’m really pleased to be returning to Power Maxed Racing and being back on the BTCC grid.

“Since moving to Australia, I’ve had some great opportunities to continue racing, but the BTCC has always been where I wanted to be competing. It’s great to be back with a team I know well, and I’m looking forward to getting started again and seeing what we can achieve together.”

title.rendered: Independents Trophy: The story so far

excerpt.rendered: Four events into the 2026 Kwik Fit British Touring Car Championship, Mikey Doble (LKQ Euro Car Parts with Power Maxed Racing) sits at the top of the Independent Drivers' Championship, holding a five-point lead over Daryl De Leon (WSR). Behind them, the fight is just as tight, with only fourteen points covering second to fifth […]

content.rendered:
Four events into the 2026 Kwik Fit British Touring Car Championship, Mikey Doble (LKQ Euro Car Parts with Power Maxed Racing) sits at the top of the Independent Drivers' Championship, holding a five-point lead over Daryl De Leon (WSR). Behind them, the fight is just as tight, with only fourteen points covering second to fifth in the standings with Aiden Moffat (LKQ Euro Car Parts with Power Maxed Racing) and Gordon Shedden (Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport) separated by just a single point.

Donington Park

Doble made the perfect start to his campaign at the opening round, taking the top spot ahead of Charles Rainford (WSR) and Shedden in a race that would see him take not only the top spot in the Independents Trophy, but the overall race win as well.

Despite the strong start for Doble, it would be Toyota Corolla GR Sport of Shedden that would take the honours in the second bout of the weekend, finishing just ahead of fellow countryman Moffat. It would be the #16 that would walk away from the Leicestershire venue in top spot, as the Scot would come home in Race 3 as the leading independent in the race and across race day.

Brands Hatch

Doble was the man to beat again in the Kent opener, crossing the line to head the independents ahead of Chris Smiley (Restart Racing). Race two brought a rain-affected contest, and it was Árón Taylor-Smith (Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport) who made the conditions count, taking both the overall and independent honours for what was his first overall win in over a decade.

The roles would reverse once more in this tight championship as Doble rose to the fore once again in the final contest of the weekend, taking his second overall podium in a strong start to the campaign. De Leon was the next independent on the road, ahead of Shedden, as LKQ Euro Car Parts with Power Maxed Racing left the shortest circuit on the calendar with a one-two in the standings.

Snetterton

The order shifted yet again at Snetterton as Rainford charged forward after the BMW 330i M Sport driver started from first in Saturday’s Race to Pole, then led from start to finish and converted pole position into WSR's first independent win of the year in Race 1.

Despite this impressive performance from the now three-time BTCC race winner, Shedden took control for the remainder of the day, winning both race two and race three - the latter the 54th overall race win of his BTCC career. Shedden's haul was enough to draw level with Doble at the head of the Independent Drivers' order heading to Cheshire with both WSR drivers within three points of the lead duo.

Oulton Park

Taylor-Smith would arrive at Oulton Park on the pace, topping qualifying to lead the Race to Pole from the front. Come race day, the #50 would take his second independent triumph of the season in the opening exchange. 

Closing out the first four events would become a WSR affair, with both De Leon and Rainford taking the top spot in the remaining two races. Even without making headlines as the mid-season break approached, Doble returned to the outright lead as Shedden fell from equalling the Audi A3 Saloon driver down to fifth.

The field is poised to return to action as the 2026 Kwik Fit British Touring Car Championship heads to Thruxton (25/26 July), with the nine drivers to have contested the Independent Trophy so far separated by less than a single event's points.

title.rendered: BTCC newcomer Lewis Gilbert to complete 2026 season with WSR

excerpt.rendered: WSR has today (13 July) announced an expanded three-car line-up for the remainder of the 2026 Kwik Fit British Touring Car Championship as Scottish single-seater racer Lewis Gilbert joins the team. Lewis, 22, joins BTCC race winners Charles Rainford and Daryl De Leon at WSR and will make his championship debut at Thruxton (25/26 July). […]

content.rendered:
WSR has today (13 July) announced an expanded three-car line-up for the remainder of the 2026 Kwik Fit British Touring Car Championship as Scottish single-seater racer Lewis Gilbert joins the team.

Lewis, 22, joins BTCC race winners Charles Rainford and Daryl De Leon at WSR and will make his championship debut at Thruxton (25/26 July).

His signing means that the 18-time series champions will run a trio of BMW 330i M Sport race cars as the push for more wins and podium finishes gathers momentum.

Lewis, from Dalry, North Ayrshire, is a European Champion kart racer, having won the Euro Series X30 Junior title in 2018 as well as the Kartmasters British GP crown in the Rotax Junior class the same year.

Those accolades came in only his second season of high-level karting. More recently, he was runner-up in a number of Senior Max class finals.

After testing F4 and Formula Regional cars, he made his car racing debut earlier this year in the GB3 Series and has recorded multiple top-ten finishes to date.

He will test his WSR BMW for the first time at Snetterton later this week before making his race debut at Thruxton the following weekend. His status as a Rookie means he will be eligible for the Jack Sears Trophy as well the overall and Independent Championships.

Lewis Gilbert said: “I’m thrilled to make my touring car debut with WSR and to join the BTCC – a series I’ve watched and loved for a long time. I’m only half a season into my car-racing career and already fortunate to be in a position to move into the BTCC, which has been a long-term aim since I started karting. WSR are the most successful team ever in the BTCC and with all the championships they won in single-seaters before that and the experience they have, I know I’m in the best place possible. I definitely feel like I’m in at the deep end with the fastest circuit on the calendar – Thruxton – first, and then my home track at Knockhill, which couldn’t be more different, straight after. But with the knowledge around me at WSR, I’m really looking forward to the challenge.”

Dick Bennetts, Team Principal, said: “We’re very pleased to welcome Lewis to WSR. Throughout our history we’ve developed a strong track record of working with young drivers and turning them into champions, whether it be Colin Turkington and Jake Hill in touring cars, or Ayrton Senna and Mika Hakkinen in Formula 3. Lewis is the latest in a long line of such young talents, and he makes the switch from single-seaters to touring cars with a huge amount of promise waiting to be untapped. He’s probably quite a new name to many BTCC supporters, but we think he’ll be a fast learner and definitely has the potential to surprise quite a few people during the second half of the year.”

title.rendered: 7-Eleven: Ricky Collard

excerpt.rendered: We turn to Ricky Collard for the next driver in our 7-Eleven series as the Team VERTU star has taken a podium at every event so far this season. The 84-time starter has stood on the podium five times in the first 12 races on his return to the BTCC, more than every driver bar […]

content.rendered:
We turn to Ricky Collard for the next driver in our 7-Eleven series as the Team VERTU star has taken a podium at every event so far this season. The 84-time starter has stood on the podium five times in the first 12 races on his return to the BTCC, more than every driver bar championship leader Ash Sutton (NAPA Racing UK) and title rival Tom Ingram (Team VERTU), yet the outright win has eluded him. Here's how he's built one of the most consistent campaigns of the season so far.

Donington Park

Collard's first podium of the year came in the reverse-grid finale. Lining up in pole position, the  #11 made a good start but had to withstand early pressure from Charles Rainford (WSR), who dived inside at the Old Hairpin to briefly take the lead before Collard reclaimed it through the Craner Curves a lap later. After leading Sutton and Dan Cammish (NAPA Racing UK) for several laps, the four-time champion found a way past on lap nine as his teammate and Ingram also found a way through by lap 13. Collard held on for third at the flag as Cammish had a ten-second time penalty for exceeding track limits applied to his race time, his first podium since returning to the championship and only the third of his BTCC career.

Brands Hatch Indy

Collard was Sutton's closest challenger throughout the opening race of the weekend, spending long spells locked in a fight for the lead and briefly moving ahead during a thrilling mid-race sequence. The #11 ultimately had to settle for second at the flag, his best BTCC result to date.

The Hyundai i30N driver was back on the rostrum in worsening conditions in Race 2, briefly leading after a bold move on Sutton as the rain intensified, before Árón Taylor-Smith (Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport) charged through the pack to snatch a remarkable maiden win. Collard crossed the line third when the race was red-flagged after Sutton made his way back through, resulting in a third podium in four rounds.

Snetterton 

Collard's podium run continued at Snetterton in the second race on Sunday after starting from eighth, where he was promoted into the fight after Ingram retired from the lead with an alternator failure. The Frimley-born driver had already been making ground of his own, pulling off a superb move around the outside of Cammish into Brundle to move up to second, before Tom Chilton completed a Team VERTU two-three behind Sutton. Collard crossed the line 1.009 seconds behind the winner, his fourth podium of the campaign.

Oulton Park

The weekend finale brought Collard's closest attempt yet at an elusive maiden win. Starting fifth on soft tyres directly behind Rainford in third, the pair made their way to the front by lap five as Rainford swept around the outside of Chris Smiley (Restart Racing) at Cascades, with Collard following through at Lodge to seize second. The #11 held Rainford honest, but the WSR driver held firm to take the win by 1.319 seconds. Collard's second-place finish made it a podium at every event so far in 2026, his fifth of the campaign.

title.rendered: 7-Eleven: Ash Sutton

excerpt.rendered: Continuing the 7-Eleven series during the mid-season break, celebrating the seven different drivers to win a race and the 11 different drivers to reach the podium so far in 2026, we turn to the man leading the way in both categories: four-time champion Ash Sutton. The NAPA Racing UK driver has been the standout performer […]

content.rendered:
Continuing the 7-Eleven series during the mid-season break, celebrating the seven different drivers to win a race and the 11 different drivers to reach the podium so far in 2026, we turn to the man leading the way in both categories: four-time champion Ash Sutton. The NAPA Racing UK driver has been the standout performer of the opening third of the season, taking five wins and nine podiums from the first 12 races to build a commanding 48-point lead. Here's how the #116 took every one of those podiums.

Donington Park

Sutton's year got off to the unlikeliest of starts. A clash with Tom Ingram (Team VERTU) in Saturday's Qualifying Race left Sutton out of that contest and last on the grid for race day. The Ford Focus Titanium racer wasted no time in rectifying his Saturday, gaining eight places on the opening lap alone to move up to 13th, before continuing his charge through the field to cross the line fourth on the road. With Ingram later disqualified for a significant overboost infringement and Daniel Rowbottom (Cataclean Plato Racing) handed a time penalty, Sutton was promoted to second, salvaging an impressive result.

There was no stopping the #116 after that. Starting Race 2 from second on the grid, Sutton took the lead by the exit of Redgate on the opening tour and never looked back, controlling the race to the flag for his 48th career BTCC win. Sutton doubled up in Race 3, making it two wins from two to complete a perfect turnaround from his Saturday setback. The victory closed out the opening weekend of the season and marked his 300th BTCC start in style.

Brands Hatch Indy

The NAPA Racing UK driver made it three wins in a row in the opening round of the weekend, starting from pole and keeping the chasing pack at bay. Ricky Collard (Team VERTU) mounted a serious challenge in the closing laps, eventually passing Sutton for the lead on lap 12 in what developed into a top-class scrap between the pair. Sutton returned the favour just a lap later to reclaim the position and hold on for his 50th career triumph in just 301 starts.

Race 2 brought the unknown in worsening conditions. Sutton retained the lead from pole, darting clear through Druids before a first-lap safety car paused the action. As rain intensified through the second half of the race and drivers scrambled, Árón Taylor-Smith (Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport) delivered a sensational drive from 13th on the grid, catching and passing Sutton to claim a first win in ten years. Sutton crossed the line second, unable to match the pace of the flying Toyota Corolla GR Sport.

The weekend finale saw Sutton produce one of the drives of the weekend. Starting seventh, he stormed through the field to fight around the outside of polesitter James Dorlin (Restart Racing) at Paddock Hill Bend to challenge for the lead, before settling for second as Dorlin held firm out in front. Sutton held on to complete six podiums from six races to open the campaign, a perfect start that took his championship lead to 47 points heading into the season's hottest weekend so far.

Snetterton 300

Sutton answered a difficult Race to Pole in trademark style on Sunday. Starting Race 2 from 10th on the grid, the #116 carved his way forward, passing Dan Cammish (NAPA Racing UK) before setting his sights on Ingram out front. The pressure told on the approach to the Wilson hairpin, where Ingram ran deep and handed Sutton the lead. From there, the NAPA Racing UK star controlled the race to the flag, sealing a dominant win by more than six seconds, his 51st career triumph.

Race 3 brought a further podium in a frenetic finale. Sutton spent much of the race chasing down leader Gordon Shedden, closing to within a few tenths on multiple occasions, but the Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport driver held his nerve to the flag. Sutton crossed the line second to extend his championship advantage to 57 points.

Oulton Park Island

Sutton was back to his best in the second race of the day in Cheshire. Starting seventh, he was up to fifth by the end of the opening lap before passing Josh Cook (Speedworks Corolla Racing) for fourth, setting his sights on the battling leaders ahead. On lap three, the #116 used his soft-tyre advantage to sweep past title rival Ingram into Cascades, before quickly passing NAPA Racing UK teammate Cammish as the duo were on differing strategies, to a lead he would never relinquish. With a clear track in front of him, Sutton romped away to cross the line 19.333 seconds clear for his fifth win of the campaign.

Sutton sits in the summer break now with a 48-point lead over Ingram at the top of the standings, and his sights firmly set on building towards a record-breaking fifth BTCC title when the championship resumes at Thruxton (25/26 July).

title.rendered: Independent Teams Championship separated by a single point in the break

excerpt.rendered: Four events into the 2026 Kwik Fit British Touring Car Championship, LKQ Euro Car Parts with Power Maxed Racing sit in the mid-season break at the top of the Independent Teams Championship, holding a slender one-point advantage over WSR. Three different outfits have taken the chequered flag first in the class standings across the opening […]

content.rendered:
Four events into the 2026 Kwik Fit British Touring Car Championship, LKQ Euro Car Parts with Power Maxed Racing sit in the mid-season break at the top of the Independent Teams Championship, holding a slender one-point advantage over WSR. Three different outfits have taken the chequered flag first in the class standings across the opening 12 races, with the gap at the front getting ever tighter over the last two events.

Donington Park

Mikey Doble set the early pace for LKQ Euro Car Parts with Power Maxed Racing (PMR) at Donington Park, his opening race success doubling as the overall win of the round. Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport interrupted the sequence in race two, with Gordon Shedden crossing the line third overall to take the honours among the independent teams. PMR hit back in the finale, Aidan Moffat's fourth-place finish was enough to seal a second success of the weekend for the team and put it eight points clear of WSR, taking points in every round over its nearest competitor.

Brands Hatch

The pattern continued at Brands Hatch, where Doble was again the man to beat in the opener, heading home ahead of James Dorlin, whose runner-up finish marked Restart Racing's best result of the season in the independent standings. Race two brought a rain-affected contest at the Kent circuit, and it was Árón Taylor Smith who made the conditions count, taking both the overall and independent honours for Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport, the Irishman's first overall success in a decade. Doble completed the set in race three, a second triumph of the weekend and his third of the campaign so far, leaving LKQ Euro Car Parts with Power Maxed Racing on top in four of the opening six races.

Snetterton

WSR opened its account in round seven, the opening race of the Snetterton weekend. Charles Rainford converted pole position, secured after winning the prior day's Qualifying Race, into the team's first independent triumph of the year. Gordon Shedden then took control for the remainder of the day, topping both race two and race three, the latter marking the 54th overall race win of his BTCC career. With Rainford and Shedden both among the independents to take overall victory, the weekend continued the theme of an independent team taking the outright race win at every event so far this season, a feat that would continue in Cheshire.

Oulton Park

Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport made it three in a row in the first exchange at Oulton Park. On this occasion, it was Árón Taylor Smith who took the opening race of the Oulton Park weekend. It then became a WSR weekend as Rainford followed up his Snetterton pace with a second success of the season in race two, before Daryl De Leon shared the spoils in race three, the perfect run-in for the most successful outfit in BTCC history ahead of the mid-season break. Between them, Rainford and De Leon's Oulton Park results meant WSR had taken three of the six races across Snetterton and Oulton Park.

LKQ Euro Car Parts with Power Maxed Racing retains the outright advantage by the smallest of margins, its four successes from the opening two events still just enough to hold off an oncoming WSR, which has taken three of the last six races without reply from the championship leaders. Laser Tools Racing with MB Motorsport remains firmly in the conversation too; the team's five successes across the campaign so far is the most of any outfit in the championship, even if it has not yet translated into the outright lead. With the destination of the Independent Teams Championship still finely poised between all competing teams, the fight is set to continue when the Kwik Fit British Touring Car Championship resumes at Thruxton (25/26 July).

title.rendered: Jack Sears Trophy: The story so far

excerpt.rendered: The 2026 Kwik Fit British Touring Car Championship season has produced one of the most fiercely contested Jack Sears Trophy seasons, with three different drivers taking home the coveted silverware across the first four events. As the grid heads into the mid-season break, Dexter Patterson leads the way with two stellar weekends keeping him atop […]

content.rendered:
The 2026 Kwik Fit British Touring Car Championship season has produced one of the most fiercely contested Jack Sears Trophy seasons, with three different drivers taking home the coveted silverware across the first four events. As the grid heads into the mid-season break, Dexter Patterson leads the way with two stellar weekends keeping him atop the table.

Donington Park

Dexter Patterson made sure the season could not have opened in more ominous fashion for the rest of the Jack Sears Trophy field. On his first outing for Steel Seal with Power Maxed Racing, the Scot claimed a clean sweep of Jack Sears Trophy triumphs across all three races at Donington Park, in the process recording the best weekend of his BTCC career to date. A pair of seventh-place overall finishes before a sixth-place finish in the finale equalled his previous career-best of sixth, set at Knockhill back in 2023.

Behind him, Sam Osborne (NAPA Racing UK) left Leicestershire as the closest challenger, with James Dorlin only two points further back in third after an encouraging opening weekend for the Restart Racing driver.

Brands Hatch

Event 2 told a different story. Dorlin took his moment at Brands Hatch, finishing second to Osborne in the opening race of the weekend before backing it up with a class victory in both remaining races. Patterson's weekend unravelled from the start, a non-finish in race one costing him dearly and handing the Jack Sears Trophy lead to the Restart Racing driver heading into Snetterton.

Lewis Selby (NAPA Racing UK) and Nic Hamilton (Team VERTU) would also take top-three class finishes across the weekend, continuing after what was an impressive debut for Selby at Donington Park and, for Hamilton, a result that would pre-empt a sensational weekend to come in Norfolk.

Snetterton

Snetterton produced one of the most memorable scenes in recent BTCC history. Dorlin topped the opening bout, with Selby second and Patterson third, before the NAPA Racing UK driver went one better in race two to take his maiden first-place finish in the first Jack Sears Trophy, with Hamilton second and the pacesetter’s teammate Osborne third. It was the final race of the day that sealed the story with the #77 taking top spot ahead of Hamilton and Patterson, but it was the Team VERTU driver’s consistency across the day that carried him to the overall honours.

This was Hamilton’s first career podium, sealed in front of a huge and emotional Snetterton crowd, and was one of the most popular in recent memory.
